title: Apple's Next iPhone SE Launching on Wednesday - Here's What We Know
content:
Apple CEO Tim Cook teased an Apple announcement that's coming on Wednesday, February 19, and it's looking like that mystery announcement will be the next-generation iPhone SE.


We've been hearing about the ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå 4 for quite some time now, and we essentially know everything to expect. If you want a sneak peek at what's coming, read on.

Naming


Apple first introduced the ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå in 2016, and the "SE" stands for Special Edition. Apple has used the SE naming for the last three models, but it's possible that's going to change this year.

There have been rumors that the new low-cost iPhone could be called the "‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå 6E" instead of the ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå 4, but the information came from a source without a well-established track record.

The low-cost ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå will have the same chip as the iPhone 16, along with a few other ‚ÄåiPhone 16‚Äå features, so it would not be a shock for Apple to give it a new name to better tie it into current flagship models. When announcing the upcoming product launch, Cook also said "get ready to meet the newest member of the family," which could be a reference to the ‚ÄåiPhone 16‚Äå family. At the same time, "family" could also just be the ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå family.

Design


We're expecting the ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå to have an ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå 14-style design, with a 6.1-inch display. Its launch will mark the end of smaller iPhones, and 6.1 inches will be the smallest screen size that Apple offers.

While the current ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå is 4.7 inches, the size difference between the 4.7-inch model and the new 6.1-inch model isn't as extreme as you might think. The updated size will be noticeable, but getting rid of those thick bezels means there's a limited increase in physical size despite the larger display.

An ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå 4 dummy model
The ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå is 5.45 inches tall and 2.65 inches wide with a thickness of 7.3mm, and the new model will be 5.78 inches tall, 2.82 inches wide, and a thickness of 7.8mm. Weight will increase just an ounce.

Apple used to sell a 5.4-inch ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå "mini," but it was discontinued after the ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå 13 mini because it didn't sell well. In the future, Apple may appease those who want a small ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå with a clamshell device that folds in half, but rumors suggest that such a device is a year or more off.

If you want to know what the ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå will look like, take a look at the iPhone 14. It'll have the same general design with an all-display design, aluminum and glass build, mute switch, volume buttons, notch, power button, and 5G antenna/SIM tray. The ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå 4 is expected to have a single-lens rear camera, though, rather than the dual-lens setup of the ‚ÄåiPhone 14‚Äå.


You likely saw early rumors about an Action Button or Camera Control button for the ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå 4 during the rumor cycle, but it sounds like those are not features that are coming to Apple's low-cost device after all. ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå dummy models have not included an Action button or a Camera Control button, nor have cases designed for the device.

Both early ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå cases and dummy models are often crafted based on supply chain leaks, and they're usually accurate because there's a good money in having a case ready to go right when a new device launches.

Display


The ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå 12 introduced Ceramic Shield, which is stronger than typical smartphone glass and better able to hold up to drops and scratches, and it's likely the new low-cost ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå will adopt the Ceramic Shield material. The current ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå just uses a standard glass front rather than the Ceramic Shield material.

Apple is updating the ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå with an OLED display, which means the end of LCD display technology in the ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå lineup when the current model is discontinued. LCDs are still used for some Macs and iPads, but the ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå lineup will be all OLED when the SE 4 launches.


The ‚ÄåiPhone 14‚Äå had a notch to house the TrueDepth front-facing camera and facial recognition hardware, and the ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå will have the same notch design. There were rumors suggesting Apple could use the Dynamic Island for the ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå, but that's another feature that looks like it'll be reserved for the more expensive ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå options.

No More Touch ID


With an all-display design like the ‚ÄåiPhone 14‚Äå, Apple is eliminating Touch ID. When the current-generation ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå is discontinued in favor of the new model, there will be no ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå with ‚ÄåTouch ID‚Äå in the lineup.

The current ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå
‚ÄåTouch ID‚Äå is still used for Macs and some iPads, but Apple will transition fully to Face ID for its ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå models with the ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå 4.

There are people who prefer ‚ÄåTouch ID‚Äå to ‚ÄåFace ID‚Äå, but Apple is all-in on facial recognition, and it's more secure than ‚ÄåTouch ID‚Äå. There's a less than 1 in 1,000,000 chance that a random person could unlock your ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå with ‚ÄåFace ID‚Äå, but a 1 in 50,000 chance for ‚ÄåTouch ID‚Äå.

Eliminating the ‚ÄåTouch ID‚Äå home button provides much more viewable screen space, which is a major bonus. Android manufacturers have married large display sizes with fingerprint recognition by putting sensors on the back of their smartphones, but Apple has never adopted that kind of design.

No More Lightning


The ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå will have a USB-C port for charging, with Apple continuing its mission to phase out the Lightning port.


The ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå is one of the last Apple devices that continues to use Lightning, and when Apple discontinues the ‚ÄåiPhone 14‚Äå, which could happen with the ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå launch, Lightning will be eliminated from the ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå lineup. It's quite possible the ‚ÄåiPhone 14‚Äå will be discontinued when the ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå launches because it makes little sense to keep a lower spec ‚ÄåiPhone 14‚Äå around for $599 when there's a more powerful ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå available that costs less.

MagSafe


The ‚ÄåiPhone 14‚Äå added support for MagSafe, and since the ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå 4 is using an ‚ÄåiPhone 14‚Äå design, it will work with ‚ÄåMagSafe‚Äå accessories and will support ‚ÄåMagSafe‚Äå wireless charging. ‚ÄåMagSafe‚Äå is not an option for the current ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå, but ‚ÄåMagSafe‚Äå charging and ‚ÄåMagSafe‚Äå accessories have become an increasingly important part of the Apple ecosystem over the last few years.


A18 Chip and 8GB RAM


Apple plans to equip the ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå 4 with the same A18 chip that's in the ‚ÄåiPhone 16‚Äå, which means it's going to have flagship performance at a much more affordable price. The A18 chip will allow it to last for years to come, and it's going to be the most affordable way to get Apple Intelligence in a smartphone.


With the A18 chip, the ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå 4 will support ‚ÄåApple Intelligence‚Äå features, and it's going to be a huge improvement over the A15 chip in the current ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå. ‚ÄåApple Intelligence‚Äå requires 8GB RAM, so the new ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå 4 will also have double the RAM of the current model.

Storage Space


There's no concrete storage space rumor, but the rest of Apple's iPhones start with 128GB of storage, so it's quite possible we're going to see the end of the 64GB storage option this year. 64GB is simply not enough storage space, even for a low-cost ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå.

Camera


The ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå could get the same 48-megapixel Wide lens that's in the ‚ÄåiPhone 16‚Äå, which will bring a major leap in photo quality compared to the 12-megapixel camera in the ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå 3.

An ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå dummy model
The ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå 4 won't have a Telephoto lens or an Ultra Wide lens, but if it does indeed use the same Wide lens, it will support next-generation portraits with focus and depth control, the newest photographic styles, and an option to crop in to 2x optical zoom.

Connectivity


Apple is going to debut its new in-house modem in the ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå 4, and it'll give us our first look at how Apple's modem technology compares to Qualcomm's modem technology.


Apple's modem chip won't support the fastest mmWave 5G speeds, but it will connect to the more widespread 6GHz 5G networks. Apple wants to cut down on its reliance on Qualcomm, and this new modem chip is the first step.

We don't know how much Apple will promote the new modem, but expect to see a range of speed and performance comparisons where the new modem is pitted against Qualcomm's current ‚ÄåiPhone 16‚Äå modem.

Emergency SOS via Satellite and Crash Detection


This is speculation, but with the ‚ÄåiPhone 14‚Äå, Apple added a Crash Detection feature that alerts emergency services if you're in a car accident. It's an important safety feature that's likely to be included in the ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå 4.


The ‚ÄåiPhone 14‚Äå and later also support Emergency SOS via Satellite, and there's a good chance that'll be added to the ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå 4 too. Apple loves to promote its safety features, and they can be a major selling point, so we wouldn't be surprised to see both of these options.

Battery Life


Rumors suggest the ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå 4 will have the same battery as the ‚ÄåiPhone 14‚Äå, which means it'll offer up to 20 hours of video playback, up to 16 hours of streaming video, and up to 80 hours of audio playback.


There's quite a bit more space in a 6.1-inch device for a battery than there is in a 4.7-inch device, so it should come as no surprise that there will be a major improvement in overall battery life.

The current ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå offers 15 hours of video playback and up to 10 hours of streaming video playback.

Pricing


With higher-end components, the ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå 4 could be more expensive than the current model, but Apple is still aiming for a price point under $500. The ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå 3 is priced starting at $429.


No Event


The ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå 4 is an important new ‚ÄåiPhone‚Äå in Apple's lineup, but it reuses components from the ‚ÄåiPhone 14‚Äå and ‚ÄåiPhone 16‚Äå, so it's not the most exciting device. Apple isn't going to hold an event to unveil the ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå 4, instead introducing it through a press release and coverage on the Apple website.

Get ready to meet the newest member of the family.

Wednesday, February 19. #AppleLaunch pic.twitter.com/0ML0NfMedu

‚Äî Tim Cook (@tim_cook) February 13, 2025

Apple did promote the launch early on social media, but without hints about just what product is coming on February 19.

Launch Date


After being introduced on February 19, Apple will likely accept pre-orders for the ‚ÄåiPhone SE‚Äå 4 before launching it a bit later in the month.
